message:
fix(packs/system/disk): close quote-bypass on dd of=/dev/... paths

`dd of="/dev/sda" if=/dev/zero` unquotes to `dd of=/dev/sda` at exec
time, wiping the device. Our destructive `dd-device` / `dd-wipe`
patterns required a literal `/` after `of=`, so quoted forms slipped
past every time. Critical-severity rule; same quote-bypass class as the
earlier rm/chmod/chown fixes.

Added `['"]?` around `/dev/...` in:
- dd-device (Critical)
- dd-wipe (Critical)
- dd-discard safe pattern (so quoted /dev/null stays safe)
- dd-file-out safe pattern (covers `dd of="./file.img"` consistency)

Regression test confirms both quoted forms are now blocked and the
quoted /dev/null short-circuit still works.
